assumptions: Assume the declared intragroup dividend has created a recognised intragroup monetary receivable or payable.
Assume the question is about hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for a recognised asset or liability, and the context specifically addresses when intragroup monetary items may qualify as hedged items in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows of recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions, and the context includes specific consolidated-FS guidance for foreign currency risk on qualifying intragroup items and transactions.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 treat a hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements.
